full_prompt: Design a steel baseplate that anchors a tubular steel column to a cracked C20/25 normal-weight concrete slab using a single canonical adhesive-anchor family: four epoxy-acrylate adhesive anchors with M16 grade 8.8 threaded rods at an effective embedment depth h_ef = 125 mm (the catalog's canonical size for M16). The representative published characteristic resistances for this configuration in cracked C20/25 concrete per representative 2024 ETA data are approximately: characteristic tension (bond/pull-out combined) N_Rk ~= 74 kN per anchor, characteristic steel tension N_Rk,s ~= 90.4 kN per anchor, characteristic steel shear V_Rk,s ~= 54.2 kN per anchor, and characteristic concrete cone N_Rk,c depending on spacing and edge distance. The baseplate is a rectangular steel plate (S355) at least 20 mm thick, 300 x 300 mm in plan, with four anchor holes arranged on a 200 x 200 mm square pattern, centered under a welded 120 x 120 x 8 mm rectangular hollow section (RHS) column stub 300 mm tall. The anchor pattern must maintain edge distance c >= 80 mm (>= 0.5 h_ef per ETA minimum) and spacing s >= 100 mm. The baseplate, column stub, and weld detail must be modeled as a single assembly; the anchors and threaded rods are included as cylindrical bolt features with preload. Applied loads at the top of the column stub: axial tension up to 120 kN, horizontal shear up to 80 kN, and an overturning moment of 25 kN*m about the strong axis. Pass/fail is evaluated under the ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 / EOTA TR 029 framework: (R1) per-anchor tension demand <= phi * N_Rk / gamma_M with phi = 0.75 and gamma_M,p = 1.5 for adhesive bond, giving a factored tension capacity N_Rd ~= 37 kN per anchor; (R2) per-anchor shear demand <= V_Rd,s = V_Rk,s / gamma_M,s with gamma_M,s = 1.25, giving V_Rd,s ~= 43 kN per anchor; (R3) combined tension+shear interaction (N/N_Rd)^5/3 + (V/V_Rd)^5/3 <= 1.0 at every anchor; (R4) concrete breakout and pryout checks must satisfy the same gamma_M = 1.5 with the effective anchor group area A_Nc/A_Nc0; (R5) baseplate bending stress <= 0.9 * f_y = 0.9 * 355 = 319.5 MPa under the factored load combination (AISC 360-22 LRFD flexural resistance); (R6) plate deflection at the column footprint <= L/300 = 1.0 mm across the 300 mm plate width. Manufacturing: baseplate is flame-cut then machined flat to within 0.5 mm across the column footprint; holes are drilled oversize by 2 mm relative to anchor diameter. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly file with baseplate, column stub, weld fillets, and anchor envelopes; a loads/capacities report listing per-anchor demand vs capacity, combined interaction, concrete cone check, plate stress, and plate deflection.
short_prompt:
Design a steel baseplate that anchors a tubular steel column to a cracked C20/25 normal-weight concrete slab using four epoxy-acrylate adhesive anchors with M16 grade 8.8 threaded rods at effective embedment depth h_ef = 125 mm. The assembly includes the baseplate, a welded RHS column stub, and the anchor/weld detail as a single modeled assembly with preloaded cylindrical bolt features.

Envelope: rectangular S355 baseplate at least 20 mm thick, 300 x 300 mm in plan, with four anchor holes on a 200 x 200 mm square pattern centered under a welded 120 x 120 x 8 mm RHS column stub 300 mm tall. Edge distance c >= 80 mm and spacing s >= 100 mm.

Design per ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 / EOTA TR 029, with adhesive-bond partial factor gamma_M,p = 1.5 (phi = 0.75 on tension) and steel shear partial factor gamma_M,s = 1.25. Baseplate bending checked per AISC 360-22 LRFD (limit 0.9 * f_y = 319.5 MPa for S355).

Applied loads at the top of the column stub.
- Axial tension up to 120 kN.
- Horizontal shear up to 80 kN.
- Overturning moment 25 kN*m about the strong axis.

Deflection limit: L/300 = 1.0 mm across the 300 mm plate width.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Concrete cone breakout, pry-out, and combined interaction are closed-form equations from ACI 318-19 Ch.17 / EOTA TR 029, not FEA. Standard implementations are available in anchor-design software packages.

prompt.title: Adhesive-anchor steel baseplate on C20/25 concrete
prompt.brief: Design a 4-anchor steel baseplate using an epoxy-acrylate adhesive anchor system with M16 grade 8.8 rods at h_ef = 125 mm in cracked C20/25 concrete, under combined axial, shear, and moment.

boundary_conditions.concrete_interface: baseplate bearing on concrete; uplift resisted only by anchors
boundary_conditions.anchor_preload: catalog value per adhesive-anchor design software for M16 threaded rod

objective.minimize: baseplate mass subject to passing all anchor + plate checks

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: N_Rd = N_Rk / gamma_M,p = 74 / 1.5 * phi with phi=0.75 and gamma_M,p=1.5 per representative ETA + ACI 318-19 Ch.17
pass_fail_criteria.note: Representative factored capacity for adhesive anchor + M16 h_ef=125 mm, cracked C20/25.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: group N_Rd,c with A_Nc/A_Nc0 per ACI 318-19 17.4.2
L/300 with L = 300 mm plate span

requirements.governing_standard: ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 + EOTA TR 029 + AISC 360-22 + representative adhesive-anchor ETA

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static service load case; seismic anchor checks are a separate variant.

notes.numeric_source: Representative 2024 ETA values for an epoxy-acrylate adhesive anchor with M16 rod h_ef=125 mm, cracked C20/25. Exact characteristic resistances vary with bond factor; set to null and annotate if the agent needs to pull verbatim from ETA.

eval_coverage: mixed FEA + closed-form anchor design workflow
